hook: You already have an intuitive sense of chance — you know a fair coin lands heads about half the time, and that rare events are, well, rare. But intuition alone breaks down fast: why does a positive medical test not mean you're probably sick, or why do seemingly independent events secretly depend on each other? This unit builds the rigorous mathematical language that lets you reason about uncertainty with complete precision.
concept: Every topic here is about constructing and using a single, coherent framework for measuring uncertainty — starting from the formal rules that all probability must obey, then applying them to counting, updating beliefs, and decomposing events. This framework is the *probability space* $(\Omega, \mathcal{F}, P)$.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to formally define a probability space and verify that the Kolmogorov axioms underpin every probability calculation you will ever do.
Master counting techniques and the inclusion-exclusion principle to compute exact probabilities when outcomes are equally likely and events overlap.
Apply conditional probability and Bayes' Theorem to update the probability of an event given new information — the foundation of rational inference.
You will learn to rigorously define statistical independence for events and collections, and recognize when $P(A \cap B) = P(A)P(B)$ truly holds versus when it only appears to.
